anchor.snippet: Decision theory or the theory of rational choice is a branch of probability
Normative decision theory is concerned with identification of optimal decisions
descriptive decision theory is concerned with describing observed behaviors
Prescriptive decision theory is concerned with predictions about behavior
the idea of expected value is that, when faced with a number of actions
He gives an example in which a Dutch merchant is trying to decide whether to insure a cargo
proved that expected utility maximization followed from basic postulates about rational behavior
showed that human behavior has systematic and sometimes important departures from expected-utility maximization
Intertemporal choice is concerned with the kind of choice where different actions lead to outcomes that are realized at different stages over time
Heuristics are procedures for making a decision without working out the consequences of every option
the gambler's fallacy — believing that an isolated random event is affected by previous isolated random events
The compromise effect operates under a mindset that the most moderate option carries the most benefit
the complete class theorems, which show that all admissible decision rules are equivalent to the Bayesian decision rule
called the ludic fallacy , is that there are inevitable imperfections in modeling the real world by particular models
